config.method_explanation: Features are scaled to regime-specific z-scores (bull/bear separate stds), then weighted Euclidean distance is computed. Crash-era outliers are clipped at +-4 sigma. Proven +0.6pp/+1.5pp improvement on 42d/63d accuracy vs min-max.

pattern_distance.description: Cosine distance of the normalized daily-return pattern over the lookback window vs. today's pattern.
pattern_distance.why: Captures the *shape* of recent price action — the single most informative signal for finding days that 'felt' like today.
pattern_distance.change: KEPT from original (was 60% fixed weight). Now participates equally in the Euclidean distance after min-max scaling.

cum_return.description: Cumulative % return over the lookback window.
cum_return.why: Two days can have similar shapes but vastly different magnitudes; this anchors the pattern to an actual move size.

realized_vol_21d.description: 21-day annualized realized volatility (std of daily returns x sqrt(252) x 100).
realized_vol_21d.why: Volatility regime is one of the strongest conditioning variables for forward return distributions.

dist_50ma_pct.description: % distance of close from the 50-day SMA.
dist_50ma_pct.why: Measures intermediate trend positioning — how extended or compressed the market is relative to its 50-day mean.
dist_50ma_pct.change: KEPT from original (was part of 10% MA weight).

dist_200ma_pct.description: % distance of close from the 200-day SMA.
dist_200ma_pct.why: Captures long-term trend context — bull vs. bear regime.
dist_200ma_pct.change: KEPT from original (was part of 10% MA weight).

drawdown_pct.description: % drawdown from the running all-time high.
drawdown_pct.why: Markets behave differently at new highs vs. in corrections; matching drawdown depth improves analog relevance.

dist_from_20d_high_pct.description: % distance of close from the 20-day rolling high.
dist_from_20d_high_pct.why: Measures short-term exhaustion / breakout proximity. A day sitting right at the 20d high is in a different microstructure state than one 3% below it.

ret_1d.why: Short-term momentum signal. Large single-day moves often trigger mean-reversion or continuation patterns.
ret_1d.change: NEW — original only had cumulative lookback return.

ret_3d.why: Captures very-short-term momentum/mean-reversion dynamics that single-day or 10-day windows miss.

ret_5d.why: Weekly return is the most commonly studied short-horizon signal in academic factor literature.

ret_10d.why: Bridges the gap between weekly momentum and the configurable lookback pattern return.

above_20ma.description: Binary flag: 1 if close > 20-day SMA, else 0.
above_20ma.why: Defines the short-term momentum regime. Analog matches should share the same regime to be meaningful.
above_20ma.change: NEW — original had no regime flags.

above_50ma.description: Binary flag: 1 if close > 50-day SMA, else 0.
above_50ma.why: Intermediate trend regime. Combined with the 200-MA flag, this creates a 3-tier trend classification.

above_200ma.description: Binary flag: 1 if close > 200-day SMA, else 0.
above_200ma.why: Long-term bull/bear regime. Forward return distributions are materially different above vs. below the 200-MA.

rsi_14.why: Classic overbought/oversold indicator. Readings above 70 or below 30 have strong mean-reversion implications for 1-5 day horizons.

bb_pct_b.description: Bollinger Band %B: position of close within 20d bands (0=lower, 1=upper).
bb_pct_b.why: Measures short-term extension. Values near 0 or 1 indicate price is at an extreme relative to recent volatility.
